name: Marchena and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop
name_original: Desde Marchena a Fuentes de Andalucía – circular
description: This easy 28.9-mile (46.4 km) road cycling route from Marchena to Fuentes de Andalucía offers a pleasant ride through the Sevillian countrys
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Marchena, Spain, traverse a landscape characterized by expansive agricultural plains, including dry cereal lands, sunflower fields, and olive groves. The terrain features gentle undulations and some segments with more significant elevation gains. The region is also home to the Via Verde de la Campiña, providing dedicated car-free paths for cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Marchena The most popular road cycling route is La Puebla de Cazalla and Morón de la Frontera Loop, a 40.2 miles (64.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through varied agricultural scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Marchena and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop, an easy 28.9 miles (46.4 km) path. This route provides a gentler ride through the plains, connecting two towns. Local road cyclists also love the La Calera Pass – Algámitas loop from La Puebla de Cazalla, a 68.1 miles (109.6 km) trail leading through significant elevation changes, often completed in about 5 hours 27 minutes. Road cycling around Marchena is defined by agricultural vistas, olive groves, and the Via Verde de la Campiña. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Marchena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Marchena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Marchena?
A: The region around Marchena offers a wide array of cycling routes, with over 200 options in the vicinity. Komoot features 22 curated road cycling routes, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Marchena?
A: You'll encounter diverse terrain, from expansive agricultural plains with dry cereal lands, sunflower fields, and olive groves, to routes with gentle undulations and some significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Calera Pass – Algámitas loop from La Puebla de Cazalla&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Marchena?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for beginners. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995967735/marchena-and-fuentes-de-andalucia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchena and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 28.9 miles (46.4 km) and offers a gentler ride through the plains.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling routes around Marchena?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Calera Pass – Algámitas loop from La Puebla de Cazalla&lt;/a&gt; stand out. This 68.1 miles (109.6 km) trail includes significant elevation changes, providing a demanding ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Marchena?
A: Many routes around Marchena are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995612621/la-puebla-de-cazalla-and-moron-de-la-frontera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Puebla de Cazalla and Morón de la Frontera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 40.2 miles (64.7 km) circular route offering varied agricultural scenery.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along the cycling routes?
A: You'll ride through vast agricultural vistas, including dry cereal lands, sunflower fields, and extensive olive groves. Some routes also follow natural features like the Guadaira River, where you might spot eucalyptus trees, and the dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or towns to explore on the routes?
A: Yes, many routes connect to or pass through charming towns with historical significance. You can cycle to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes de Andalucía&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Puebla de Cazalla&lt;/a&gt;, both offering cultural appeal. Marchena itself has an impressive historic center, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sevilla Gate&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the Via Verde de la Campiña and how can I access it?
A: The Via Verde de la Campiña is a significant 'Greenway' offering a paved, car-free path for cyclists. It's the second-longest greenway in Andalusia and forms part of circular routes from Marchena, such as the 'Fuentes de Andalucía from Marchena' loop. You can access a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;section of the Vía Verde between Fuentes de Andalucía and Marchena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Marchena?
A: The road cycling routes in Marchena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied agricultural scenery, the dedicated car-free paths of the Via Verde, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Marchena?
A: May and August are popular months for road cycling in Marchena. The region generally offers pleasant conditions for riding, though it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts for your specific travel dates.

Q: Are there any routes that connect Marchena to other major cities?
A: Marchena serves as a hub for longer cycling journeys. Routes connect the town to Fuentes de Andalucía and Écija. There's also a route tracing a portion of the Camino Mozárabe towards Mérida, and a historic path from Seville to Marchena, traversing through dry cereal lands and olive groves.
tips: Marchena was declared a Historic-Artistic Site in 1966, among the monuments that stand out are: the church of San Juan Bautista (one of the most interesting in the province of Seville, Gothic-Mudejar style with Renaissance elements) and the Puerta de Sevilla also called the Arco de la Rosa (one of the access doors that the walled perimeter of Marchena had).
If you are in Fuentes de Andalucía, take a walk through its alleys to see manor houses - beautiful examples of Andalusian manor architecture from the 18th century.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The circular route from Marchena to Fuentes de Andalucía is approximately 46 km long with around 270 meters of elevation gain. Most cyclists can expect to complete it in about 3 to 3.5 hours, depending on their pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, making it suitable for road cycling. However, it's important to note that sections, particularly along the Vía Verde de la Campiña, may consist of compacted soil or gravel tracks. While generally smooth, be prepared for some unpaved segments.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this route as 'easy,' but given its length and the presence of some unpaved sections, it's best suited for cyclists with a moderate level of fitness. Beginners comfortable with longer distances and varied terrain might find it enjoyable, but it's not a purely flat, paved ride.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: As a circular route, you can start in either Marchena or Fuentes de Andalucía. Both towns offer parking options, typically in municipal areas or near the town centers, which serve as convenient starting points for your ride.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the picturesque Sevillian countryside, characterized by vast crop fields and a rich historical landscape. The route passes through charming towns like Marchena and Fuentes de Andalucía, offering glimpses of agricultural life and historical remains. A significant part of the experience is cycling along sections of the Vía Verde de la Campiña, an old railway line transformed into a greenway.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in Andalusia is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter.

faqs.question: Does the route follow any official greenways or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, a notable aspect of this route is its utilization of sections of the Vía Verde de la Campiña. This greenway follows the path of the historical 'Marchenilla' railway line, blending industrial heritage with natural beauty. Other smaller sections may include parts of the Cordel de Jarda and Vereda de San Juan.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally permitted on many Vías Verdes in Spain, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in populated areas or near agricultural land. Be mindful of other cyclists and any local regulations in Marchena and Fuentes de Andalucía.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. The Vía Verde de la Campiña and other public roads are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Marchena and Fuentes de Andalucía are towns with various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can find food, drinks, and other supplies. It's a good idea to carry some water and snacks, especially for the sections between towns.

faqs.question: What type of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mix of paved roads and some unpaved sections along the Vía Verde de la Campiña, a gravel bike or a road bike with slightly wider, more robust tires would offer the most comfortable and efficient ride. A pure road bike might find the unpaved sections a bit challenging.
